Meta.Documentation:
Expression to evaluate: Syntax: expression ::= "(" expression ")" | function "(" expression ("," expression)* ")" | value | expression infix expression | pathref function ::= builtin | userdefined builtin ::= "EVAL" | "LEN" | "NOT" | "CONCAT" | "PROPERTY" | "ISNULL" | "MATCH" | "FILTER" | "MAP" infix ::= "+" | "-" | "*" | "/" | "<" | ">" | ">=" | "<=" | "==" | "!=" | "=~" | "~=" | "AND" | "&&" | "OR" | "||" value ::= "\"" string "\"" | number | "true" | "false" pathref ::= "$" integer
Quotations and backslashes in strings have to be escaped with backslash number can be float (i.e. negative and with negative exponent. Example: -3.4e-4)
EVAL([<json> | %<number>, ] pathref) EVAL expects either one argument as pathref ($n referring to the n-th entry of the array of json paths) or a json as an additional first argument. If no json is provided, the default json is used to evaluate the expression. If used inside FILTER/MAP, %1 can be used to specify the json to be the array-element, the function is iterating over. Nesting: If the _second_ argument of FILTER/MAP contains other FILTER/MAP invocations, this number represents the nesting-depth. %0 refers to the global input json, %1 to the json of the FILTER/MAP iteration, %2 to the same if nested once, and so on. Example: COUNT(FILTER(EVAL("$.list"), EVAL(%1, "$.attr")=="val"))) -> Count the number of array elements under $.list that fulfill .attr="val"
LEN returns the length of the argument, either length of array or length of string.
NOT negates the argument, assuming it is of type (or convertable to) boolean
CONCAT concatenates all arguments as strings. concats all array elements of arguments that are arrays.
PROPERTY<propertyName>[, <defaultValue>]) PROPERTY returns the value of the xyna property given by the first argument. A second argument is optional for the default value to be returned if the property is not set (or null).
ISNULL(<value>, <substitution value>) ISNULL replaces the value with the substitution value if the value is null.
MATCH(<value>, <regex>) Matches the value against the regular expression, returns true or false. Is the same as <value> =~ <regex> and <regex> ~= <value>
FILTER(<array>, <boolean expression>) returns new array of the elements for which the expression is true MAP(<array>, <expression>) returns new array consisting of evaluating expression for each input array element For both FILTER and MAP see EVAL(%<number>, ) syntax for how to access the array elements in the expression.
Operator precedence of infix functions: ("(",")") (*/) (+-) (>,...) (==,!=,~=,=~) (AND,&&) (OR,||)
Using AND/OR/&&/|| the second Argument is evaluated lazily.
Userdefined functions can be integrated via workflows: Define workflows in the package given by the xyna property xfmg.tmf.validation.constraint.function.package. Their simple name is then the name of the function that can be used in expressions. The workflow has to have a single base.Text output and either 1-4 base.Text inputs or a base.Text list as input. The workflow has to be reachable via runtime context dependencies from the root order context of the order evaluating the expression.
